name: Fellowship
description_short: Fellowship Credit Union is a not-for-profit, member-owned financial institution serving 32 Colorado counties since 1959, offering savings, checking, loans, credit cards, and mortgages with federally-insured deposits.
description_long: Fellowship Credit Union was established in 1959 and has grown through strategic mergers with East Prowers Federal Credit Union (1986), St. Francis Credit Union (2006), Arkansas Valley Federal Credit Union (2017), and most recently Otero County Teachers Federal Credit Union (2024). The credit union operates across 32 Colorado counties including Alamosa, Archuleta, Baca, Bent, Cheyenne, Conejos, Costilla, Crowley, Custer, Dolores, Elbert, Gunnison, Hinsdale, Huerfano, Kiowa, Kit Carson, Lake, Las Animas, Lincoln, Logan, Mineral, Ouray, Otero, Park, Phillips, Prowers, Rio Grande, Saguache, San Juan, San Miguel, Sedgwick, Washington, and Yuma Counties. Fellowship Credit Union offers a comprehensive range of deposit and lending products typical of full-service credit unions. These include savings and checking accounts, share certificates, investments, auto loans (featuring rates as low as 5.49% for 36-month terms), Visa credit cards (as low as 6.90% APR), mortgages, business loans, and agriculture loans. Members can also access online banking, ATM networks, and membership applications through their website. The credit union distinguishes itself through the traditional credit union philosophy of "people helping people"—being member-owned and not-for-profit rather than shareholder-driven allows them to return value to members through competitive rates and lower fees. Additionally, Fellowship operates an Education Foundation that provides scholarships to graduating seniors and college students, demonstrating community commitment. The credit union is federally-insured by the National Credit Union Administration (NCUA), operates under NMLS ID# 854270, and complies with Fair Housing Law and Equal Opportunity Credit Act standards. For consumers seeking traditional financial services in rural and semi-rural Colorado communities, Fellowship Credit Union provides established, stable access to credit union membership with modern online capabilities.
pros: Federally-insured deposits through NCUA, protecting member funds up to standard limits
Competitive auto loan rates as low as 5.49% APR for 36-month terms
Visa credit card available at 6.90% APR or lower
Member-owned, not-for-profit structure means profits are returned to members through better rates and lower fees
Education Foundation provides annual scholarships to graduating seniors and college students in service area
Expansive geographic service area covering 32 Colorado counties with multiple physical locations
Offers full suite of services including mortgages, business loans, and agriculture loans alongside consumer products
Website accessibility features including AudioEye toolbar support for members with disabilities
cons: Limited to specific geographic area (32 Colorado counties); membership restricted by county residence or employment requirements
Savings account rate of only 0.10% APY is significantly below market rates offered by online banks and some competitors
Website content does not provide full fee schedules, minimum balance requirements, or detailed terms for all products
No mention of mobile app availability or mobile banking capabilities despite online banking offering
Physical branch presence may be limited given rural/semi-rural service area, potentially inconvenient for some members
best_for: Residents or employees in the 32-county service area of south-central and eastern Colorado seeking traditional banking services
Members interested in community-focused credit union philosophy with local decision-making and Education Foundation support
Borrowers seeking competitive auto loan rates and small business owners in the service area needing agricultural or business financing
Consumers prioritizing NCUA federal insurance and member-owned governance over for-profit bank structures
diagnosis: Fellowship Credit Union is best for individuals living or working in its 32-county Colorado service area who value member-owned, not-for-profit financial institutions with established community roots and competitive lending rates. The primary caveat is strict geographic eligibility requirements and limited online competitiveness on savings rates, making it most suitable for borrowing needs rather than high-yield deposit savings.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Fellowship — Credit Union in Lamar, CO | CreditDoc
meta_description: Fellowship — NCUA-insured credit union in Lamar, CO. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
